description:
You are a Linux systems engineer beginning a hardening pass on an application host.  
For the next code-review meeting you need to provide the exact iptables rule that will be used to allow incoming HTTPS-API traffic on TCP port 8443.

Your assignment:

1. Create a new file at  
   /home/user/firewall_rule_8443.txt

2. Write **exactly one** line into that file—the rule itself—and nothing else.  
   Required line (including spelling, case, spacing, and option order):

iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8443 -j ACCEPT

3. Be sure the file ends with a single trailing newline character.

4. Do not insert a she-bang, comments, or any additional blank lines.  
   The automated test will fail if there is any deviation (extra spaces, different port, additional lines, etc.).

5. You may accomplish this with a single terminal command of your choice (e.g., using echo or printf), but that command itself will not be checked—only the final state of the file.

Verification (performed by the grader):

• The file /home/user/firewall_rule_8443.txt exists.  
• The file contains exactly the one required line followed by a newline.  
• No other content is present.

Example of what the test will read:

cat -A /home/user/firewall_rule_8443.txt  
→ should show:  
iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8443 -j ACCEPT$

Proceed to the terminal and create the file accordingly.
truth:
Path that must exist after completion:
/home/user/firewall_rule_8443.txt

Exact content (including the trailing newline, represented here as \n):

iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 8443 -j ACCEPT\n
name: task_000000_78b6f2fb
